key_insight: Large language models can sound clinically sound yet fail by not seeking the right information under uncertainty, which limits diagnostic and treatment accuracy in agentic oncology tasks.
clinical_relevance: For precision oncology, the key challenge is not just medical knowledge but whether a model can actively request molecular and cytogenetic data needed to support treatment decisions.
method: Built an agentic evaluation framework for hematologic oncology.
Required models to request clinical data over three sequential rounds before giving a diagnosis and treatment plan.
Evaluated 32 frontier models on information-seeking, reasoning traces, and final accuracy.
evidence_signal: Across 32 frontier models, the best achieved 68% overall accuracy, and information utilization strongly predicted accuracy (R = 0.69, P < 0.001).
watch_item: High-quality reasoning traces did not reliably match correct answers, so deploying these systems without robust information-seeking safeguards could lead to confident but incomplete clinical decisions.
paper_title: Information-seeking failures of large language models in agentic clinical reasoning
